seo.title: Dr. Sade Iriah | Behavioral Neuroscientist & Performance Consultant
seo.description: Dr. Sade Iriah is a behavioral neuroscientist and former Division I athlete who helps elite performers, teams, and high achievers optimize their minds for peak performance, resilience, and recovery.

heroTitle: Training the Mind Behind Elite Performance.
heroSubtitle: Grounded in science, built for competition, resilience, and mastery.
heroBody: I help athletes, teams, and high performers understand the brain so they can perform with clarity, resilience, and longevity.
whoIWorkWithTitle: Performance Demands More. I Work With Those Who Lead.

aboutPreviewBody:
Sade Iriah, PhD, is a behavioral neuroscientist and former Division I athlete who understands what it takes to compete at the highest level—and what happens when the mind becomes the barrier.

She has spent over a decade translating neuroscience research into practical frameworks for athletes, teams, and high performers. Her work bridges the gap between laboratory science and real-world application.
libraryPreviewBody: A curated collection of research and resources that shape how we understand performance, the mind, and human potential.

pageSubtitle: A Behavioral Neuroscientist and Former Division I Athlete
introText: Sade Iriah, PhD, is a behavioral neuroscientist and performance consultant who has spent over a decade translating complex brain science into real-world strategies for elite performers. A former Division I athlete herself, she understands the demands of high-stakes competition—and what happens when the mind becomes the barrier to breakthrough.

bioSections.title: The Science
bioSections.content: With expertise in neuroscience, psychology, and performance, Dr. Iriah brings a unique perspective to understanding how the brain drives—or derails—performance under pressure. Her research has explored anxiety in athletes, reward sensitivity in injury rehabilitation, and neural correlates of resilience.

bioSections.title: The Experience
bioSections.content: Beyond the lab, Dr. Iriah has worked directly with athletes across sports—from collegiate competitors navigating performance anxiety to professionals recovering from career-altering injuries. She has consulted with teams, spoken at conferences, and collaborated with organizations seeking to build cultures of mental resilience.

bioSections.title: The Mission
bioSections.content: Her mission is simple: make brain science accessible and actionable. Whether through one-on-one consulting, team workshops, or her growing Performance Library, Dr. Iriah is committed to helping people understand their minds—and use that understanding to perform at their best.

philosophyStatement: Great performance isn't luck. It's understanding. I believe that when you understand how your brain works—how it processes stress, builds habits, and recovers from setbacks—you gain the power to train it intentionally. My approach combines rigorous neuroscience with the practical wisdom that comes from competing at the highest levels.
ctaBody: Whether you're an athlete seeking mental edge, a team building resilience, or an organization investing in performance, I'd love to hear from you.

pageSubtitle: Have a question, collaboration idea, or want to book me to speak? I'd love to hear from you.
introCopy: Fill out the form below and I'll get back to you within 48 hours.

title: Athlete Performance Consulting
description: One-on-one consulting for athletes seeking to optimize mental performance, manage competition anxiety, and build psychological resilience. From pre-game preparation to injury recovery.

title: Mental Resilience & Injury Psychology
description: Evidence-based support for athletes navigating the psychological challenges of injury, rehabilitation, and return-to-play. Grounded in neuroscience research on recovery and adaptation.

title: Speaking & Workshops
description: Keynotes, team workshops, and organizational training on the neuroscience of performance, resilience under pressure, and building high-performance cultures.

title: Trait Anxiety and Attentional Control in Collegiate Athletes
abstract: This study examined the relationship between trait anxiety and attentional control in Division I collegiate athletes, exploring how individual differences in anxiety affect performance under competitive pressure.

title: Reward Sensitivity and Injury Rehabilitation Outcomes
abstract: Research investigating how individual differences in reward sensitivity predict adherence to rehabilitation protocols and psychological outcomes during injury recovery in athletes.

title: Mental Skills Training in High-Contact Sports
abstract: A comprehensive review of mental skills training interventions designed for athletes in high-contact sports, with emphasis on building resilience and managing performance anxiety.

title: Neural Correlates of Resilience in Elite Athletes
abstract: Neuroimaging study exploring the brain structures and connectivity patterns associated with psychological resilience in elite-level competitors across multiple sports.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: The definitive guide to building good habits and breaking bad ones. Clear's framework for tiny changes that lead to remarkable results is essential reading for anyone pursuing sustained excellence.

title: Mindset: The New Psychology of Success
author: Carol S. Dweck
description: Dweck's groundbreaking research on fixed vs. growth mindset has transformed how we understand learning, resilience, and achievement across sports, business, and life.

title: The Talent Code
author: Daniel Coyle
description: An exploration of how deep practice, ignition, and master coaching create world-class performers. Coyle reveals the neuroscience behind skill acquisition and talent development.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: Nobel laureate Kahneman's masterwork on the two systems that drive how we think—and how understanding them can improve decision-making under pressure.

title: Focus: The Hidden Driver of Excellence
author: Daniel Goleman
description: Goleman explores the science of attention and its critical role in performance, leadership, and achievement. Essential reading for anyone seeking sustained concentration.
